rulings.Case Title: LOUIS CASTAGNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Swinerton Builders FKA SWINERTON & WALBERG CO.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication on the sole issue of punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant and defendant¿s liability for punitive damages. The hearing is continued to September 10 on the court¿s own motion. Any party contesting the tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm today. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS CASTAGNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS CASTAGNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to any asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration and to ¶¶ 6 an 8 of Mr. Castagna¿s declaration are sustained. The hearing is continued to September 10 on the court¿s own motion. Any party contesting the tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm today. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE STEWART VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Considered together, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories are not factually devoid on this issue. The hearing is continued to September 10 on the court¿s own motion. Any party contesting the tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm today. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD EISELE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Defendant Graybar Electric Company Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2 (strict liability) and granted as to issues 3-11(false representation, concert of action, fraud, deceit/negligent misrepresentation and fraud, deceit/concealment and punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition is not factually devoid on this issue. Plaintiff did not opposed summary adjudication on issues 3-11. The hearing is continued to September 10 on the court¿s own motion. Any party contesting the tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm today. =(220/HEK)
